---
title: "Contraction Metric Neural-Network Quadrotor Controller"
author: "egmc"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
    geometry: margin=1in
execute:
  echo: false
---

# Overview

This document describes the contraction-metric tracking controller implemented
in `contraction_controller_px4`.  The controller combines a learned
neural-network policy with a linear stabilising gain to produce a tracking law
that is provably contracting along any feasible reference trajectory.

# System Model

The quadrotor state is represented as a 10-dimensional vector

$$
x = \bigl[p_x,\; p_y,\; p_z,\; v_x,\; v_y,\; v_z,\; a_z,\; \phi,\; \theta,\; \psi\bigr]^\top \in \mathbb{R}^{10},
$$

where $(p_x, p_y, p_z)$ is the NED position, $(v_x, v_y, v_z)$ the body-frame
velocity, $a_z$ the mass-normalised collective thrust ($\approx g = 9.81\,\text{m/s}^2$ at hover),
and $(\phi, \theta, \psi)$ the roll-pitch-yaw Euler angles.

The control input is

$$
u = \bigl[T,\; p_{\text{cmd}},\; q_{\text{cmd}},\; r_{\text{cmd}}\bigr]^\top \in \mathbb{R}^4,
$$

where $T$ is the collective thrust in Newtons and
$(p_{\text{cmd}}, q_{\text{cmd}}, r_{\text{cmd}})$ are body-rate commands in
rad/s.

# Neural-Network Policy

The neural network $\pi_\theta : \mathbb{R}^{10} \to \mathbb{R}^4$ is a
fully-connected network with architecture

$$
10 \xrightarrow{\text{softplus}} 128 \xrightarrow{\text{softplus}} 128
\xrightarrow{} 4,
$$

trained using contraction-metric certificates (CCM / ControlNet).  The weights
are stored in `Controller 1/Controller/model.eqx` and the architecture
descriptor in `arch.txt`.  They are loaded at runtime via

```python
control_net = irx.NeuralNetwork('Controller 1/Controller')
```

# Stabilising Gain at Equilibrium

The equilibrium state is

$$
x_{\text{eq}} = \bigl[0,0,0,\;0,0,0,\;9.81,\;0,0,0\bigr]^\top.
$$

A linear gain $K_{\text{eq}} \in \mathbb{R}^{4 \times 10}$ is added around
equilibrium to close the loop in case the network output alone does not
drive the error to zero.  The closed-loop policy at a single state is

$$
\boxed{
\texttt{control}(x,\,\pi_\theta)
  \;=\; \pi_\theta(x) + K_{\text{eq}}\,\bigl(x - x_{\text{eq}}\bigr).
}
$$

The gain $K_{\text{eq}}$ was computed offline as part of the contraction
certificate synthesis and is hard-coded in `controller.py`.

# Tracking Control Law

Let $x_{ff}(t)$ be the *feedforward state* on the reference trajectory at
time $t$, and $u_{ff}(t)$ the feedforward control that sustains that
trajectory.  The full tracking input applied to the vehicle is

$$
\boxed{
u(t)
  = \underbrace{\texttt{control}\!\bigl(x(t),\,\pi_\theta\bigr)}_{\text{feedback at current state}}
  - \underbrace{\texttt{control}\!\bigl(x_{ff}(t),\,\pi_\theta\bigr)}_{\text{feedback at reference}}
  + \underbrace{u_{ff}(t)}_{\text{feedforward}}.
}
$$

## Intuition

- **Feedback at $x(t)$** drives the vehicle towards the equilibrium manifold.
- **Feedback at $x_{ff}(t)$** cancels the nominal action along the reference so
  the feedforward alone sustains the trajectory when $x = x_{ff}$.
- **$u_{ff}(t)$** injects the forces required to follow the reference (e.g.,
  hover thrust $mg$).

This structure is the *contraction-based tracking* decomposition: the error
$e = x - x_{ff}$ is governed by a contracting differential system, guaranteeing
exponential convergence $\|e(t)\| \le c\,e^{-\lambda t}\|e(0)\|$ for some
rate $\lambda > 0$.

## Reference Construction

The reference trajectory is supplied by the `quad_trajectories` package.  The
reference state is

$$
x_{ff}(t) = \bigl[p_{x,r},\; p_{y,r},\; p_{z,r},\;
              \dot{p}_{x,r},\; \dot{p}_{y,r},\; \dot{p}_{z,r},\;
              g,\; 0,\; 0,\; \psi_r\bigr]^\top,
$$

and the feedforward input is

$$
u_{ff}(t) = \bigl[m g,\; 0,\; 0,\; 0\bigr]^\top
$$

(hover-force approximation valid for slow or flat trajectories).

# ROS 2 Integration

The node `ContractionOffboardControl` runs at **100 Hz** and follows the same
offboard flight phases as the Newton-Raphson controller:

| Phase | Duration | Control signal |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| HOVER | 10 s cushion | Position setpoint at hover height |
| CUSTOM | flight period | Contraction controller (body rates) |
| RETURN | 10 s cushion | Position setpoint at hover height |
| LAND | until touchdown | Position setpoint then land command |

The node subscribes to `/fmu/out/vehicle_odometry` to obtain $x(t)$ and
publishes to `/fmu/in/vehicle_rates_setpoint`.

# Running the Controller

See `README.md` in the package root for full make-command instructions.

```bash
# Inside the container – sim example
ros2 run contraction_controller_px4 run_node \
    --platform sim --trajectory circle_horz
```
